SDK

The interface and design decisions behind the Gemini AI client.

Architecture

The SDK wraps the Google Gemini SDK (google-genai) with a clean Python interface. It handles API key management, caching, error recovery, and model selection.

It was extracted from the Alma project. The original code lived inside a Flask application with hardcoded model names, framework coupling, and a Go service dependency. The extraction removed all of that.

Design decisions

  • Configurable models, model names are passed via a ModelConfig dataclass, not hardcoded as module constants. Different products can use different models.
  • Error recovery, _is_recoverable_api_error catches all exceptions except programming errors (TypeError, NameError, etc.). The SDK returns synthetic text for API failures instead of raising. Real bugs still propagate.
  • Empty response fallback, every method checks for empty response.text. The Gemini SDK returns empty strings without raising. The SDK returns a placeholder instead of failing silently.
  • Optional Redis, caching works with Redis or in-memory. No hard dependency on Redis.
  • No framework, no Flask, no Django, no FastAPI. Just Python classes and functions.

Methods

generate_text(prompt)              # Simple text
generate_chat(messages)            # Chat with history
generate_text_with_thinking(p)     # Text + reasoning
generate_chat_with_thinking(m)     # Chat + reasoning
generate_text_with_url_context(p)  # Text + URL context
generate_chat_with_url_context(m)  # Chat + URL context
text_to_speech(text)               # Text to MP3
generate_image(prompt)             # Image generation
research_topic(topic)              # Deep research

Image providers

Three providers via ImageProviderRegistry:

  • Gemini, default. Uses gemini-2.5-flash-image.
  • Vertex, Google Cloud Vertex AI. Requires google-cloud-aiplatform.
  • Mock, generates a red 64x64 PNG for testing.

Found something unexpected? Report an issue.
